semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all twenty-one exact source mappings, the generation-mode schema, Project Theseus import and replay artifacts, the deterministic four-task bundle, the selected support-lane aggregate, all five proof targets and thirty-eight Fast Generation declarations, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, the test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep made request-specific accelerated-route admission and end-to-end speed-receipt custody the distinct owner; expanded the contract to eighteen lifecycle mechanisms, twelve interfaces, eighteen invariants, and eighteen failure modes; classified the formal surface as five direct predicate or projection checks, thirty-one derived finite route, negative, or fixture consequences, and two authored-summary bridges; specified strong matched baselines, joint useful-quality-safety-rights-latency-resource-fairness-recovery gates, causal ablations, independent reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Natural and adversarial generation requests for declared consumers and permitted uses across chat, code, retrieval, extraction, long-context, multilingual, accessibility, agentic, artifact-producing, and high-risk workloads.
scope.environment: Twenty-one exact source mappings; one not-run schema fixture; one 18-mode/13-comparison static import with five speed-lift warnings, zero task-passing or promotable comparisons, and six invalid controls; one two-command/ten-digest replay; one four-task deterministic accounting bundle; one four-command/sixteen-digest/68-task aggregate with fourteen rejected controls and two no-change decisions; and thirty-eight finite declarations. No live decoder or serving system is represented.
scope.model: A consumer-, workload-, model-, hardware-, serving-policy-, and time-window-relative admission relation over immutable route identity, exact context and cache state, separate output states, complete queue-to-delivery timing and total-burden accounting, bounded verification, executable fallback, causal comparison, expiry, rollback, and residual custody.
scope.authority: This chapter owns request-specific accelerated-generation admission and the complete end-to-end speed receipt only. Intent, Planning, Context, Routing, Readiness, Runtime, Resource, Security, Authority, Rights, Verification, Artifact, Labor, Benchmark, Claim, Human, Incident, and Release owners retain their boundaries.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; model, tokenizer, draft, verifier, workload, prompt, context, cache, hardware, compiler, runtime, serving, load, policy, rights, threat, incident, or time changes expire affected qualifications.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; twenty-one mappings; schema fixture; Theseus import, replay, task-bundle, aggregate, transitions, and digests; five proof targets; thirty-eight declarations; and the future natural causal-transfer campaign.

assumptions: Consumers, permitted uses, tasks, distributions, model and tokenizer versions, drafts, verifiers, hardware, runtimes, serving policies, cache states, loads, output states, clocks, resources, rights, failures, and downstream outcomes can be versioned and observed without collapsing their owners.

Strong optimized autoregressive, ordinary-serving, speculative, multi-token, multi-head, feature, lookahead, early-exit, recurrent or state-space, KV-cache, diffusion, hybrid, conservative-slow, and governed baselines can run under matched conditions.
Independent-enough evaluators can measure useful accepted success, quality, safety, rights, selective risk, latency, tail behavior, resources, fairness, fallback, recovery, and residuals.
Queueing, warmup, cache effects, failures, OOMs, retries, rejected drafts, disagreements, abstentions, fallbacks, human work, and censored attempts remain in complete denominators.
Causal interventions, independent implementations, and heterogeneous transfer settings can be frozen prospectively before outcome-bearing execution.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Optimized autoregressive decoding and ordinary serving systems already provide the best quality-equivalent end-to-end tradeoff for the relevant workloads; a governed multi-mode controller adds selection, verification, metadata, fallback, and human cost without improving the joint frontier.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Under matched natural workloads, models, hardware, serving conditions, load, cache, evaluators, resources, retries, and time, the full controller fails the preregistered joint useful-quality-safety-rights-latency-resource-fairness-recovery frontier; its causal signatures do not appear; or it hides failures, verifier dependence, repair, fallback, queueing, resource, rights, tail, or transfer burden.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze the strongest current decoding, recurrent, diffusion, cache, and serving comparators without importing source-reported gains.
Justify consumer policy, quality and safety gates, rights, high-risk overrides, fairness, refusal, fallback, expiry, rollback, and residual ownership.
Specify output-state refinement, timing boundaries, route qualification, verifier dependence, fallback liveness, load fairness, expiry, and countermodels beyond trusted Booleans.
Run actual target and accelerated models, serving engines, clocks, verifiers, repair, fallback, load, failure, recovery, and rejecting controls.
Measure useful accepted success, quality, safety, selective risk, latency distributions, throughput, full resources, fairness, rights, fallback, recovery, and total burden jointly.
Ablate mode selection, accepted-output accounting, verifier independence, cache matching, full timing, repair, fallback, resources, high-risk override, load isolation, and expiry.
Reproduce independently and transfer across models, workloads, languages, contexts, hardware, runtimes, organizations, rights regimes, attacks, load states, failures, updates, and time.

contrary_evidence: The Project Theseus import records five accepted-span speed-lift warnings but zero task-passing comparisons, zero useful-solution evidence, and zero promotable comparisons.
The four-task local result uses deterministic templates and cost units, not a language model, wall clock, serving engine, hardware meter, or natural workload.
The 176-unit latency-only route is cheaper than the selected 264-unit route but fails verifier, fallback, residual, and support boundaries, showing why proxy speed is insufficient.
The replay and aggregate rerun local validators and bind digests; they do not rerun Project Theseus or establish model behavior.
Thirty-eight declarations are finite predicates, routes, fixture consequences, and authored-summary bridges; they do not establish decoder, verifier, serving, fallback, or transfer adequacy.
No current result independently reproduces useful end-to-end model speed or transfers across models, workloads, hardware, serving engines, load, or time.
known_confounds: Warmup, cache, batching, compilation, quantization, context length, output length, tenant mix, scheduler priority, and hardware occupancy can dominate measured speed.
Candidate routes may receive more engineering, verifier capacity, retries, fallback, or human review than baselines.
Generator, draft, verifier, evaluator, runtime, serving engine, and organization may share data, code, infrastructure, or failure modes.
Selective coverage and fallback can preserve quality while hiding refusal, latency, displaced capacity, and opportunity cost.
Short horizons can miss drift, tail failures, cache invalidation, incidents, rights harms, energy burden, and stale qualification.
Failed, cancelled, OOM, rejected, disputed, abstaining, or fallback attempts can disappear from denominators.
acceptance_criteria: Every lifecycle stage from scope freeze through expiry and rollback has an exact producer, consumer, valid case, rejecting mutation, consequence, recovery path, and residual owner.

A real reference slice runs actual models and serving, closes queue-to-delivery timing, independently verifies output, exercises repair and fallback under failure, attributes all resources, and retains complete attempts.

A preregistered natural and adversarial campaign clears joint usefulness, quality, safety, rights, latency, tail, resource, fairness, fallback, recovery, causal, independent-reproduction, and transfer gates against strong matched controls.

Each signature mechanism produces its predicted benefit or failure under matched ablation without explanation by cache, warmup, extra resources, weak baselines, shared evaluators, or censored failures.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact consumer, use, workload, model/tokenizer, draft, verifier, hardware, runtime, serving, load, cache, context, output length, metric, resource, rights, and horizon.
Separate record validity, proxy cost, decoder speed, accepted-output speed, useful task success, serving throughput, safety, rights, recovery, deployment, and SOTA.
Unknown verifier independence, full timing, failure denominator, fallback execution, rights, tail, resource, reproduction, or transfer remains residual or blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable trace satisfies its premises and violates state separation, timing closure, accounting, fallback, high-risk, fairness, expiry, denominator, or scope obligations.

Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves or improves the joint frontier or the apparent gain is explained by cache, warmup, extra resources, weak baselines, shared evaluation, or censored failures.
Refute useful, general, verifier-preserving, resource-efficient, serving, transferable, or SOTA language when a simpler control wins or independent reproduction and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise generation-admission owner supersedes the atom and every consumer lease, route, trace, qualification, fallback, incident, residual, descendant, and transition migrates with preserved lineage.

reproduction.command: python3 scripts/validate_theseus_generation_mode_import.py && python3 scripts/validate_theseus_support_replay_probe.py && python3 scripts/validate_fast_generation_task_bundle.py && python3 scripts/validate_theseus_fast_support_lane.py && cd lean && lake build AsiStackProofs.FastGeneration
reproduction.environment_lock: Repository static imports, deterministic fixtures, validators, and finite Lean module current through 2026-07-14; real model, serving, clock, hardware, and natural-workload campaign not frozen.
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Current validators are local deterministic implementations; independent generator, verifier, evaluator, serving, resource, rights, and measurement implementations are required for promotion.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Build the real model and serving reference slice, preregister natural workloads and strong baselines, close queue-to-delivery and total-burden accounting, exercise failure and fallback, run causal ablations, independently reproduce all implementations, and transfer across heterogeneous settings.

non_claims: A source mapping, schema, template, cost unit, speed-lift warning, import, digest, validator, theorem, or green build does not establish model speed, useful-solution-per-second improvement, quality preservation, serving superiority, deployment, transfer, or SOTA.
The current static import and deterministic bundle are bounded accounting and negative-promotion evidence only.
No support, deployment, release, AGI, or ASI state changes through this semantic review.

fast-generation-architectures.core.strongest_counterclaim: Optimized autoregression and ordinary serving already dominate at lower governance cost on the exact useful-quality-resource frontier.
fast-generation-architectures.core.falsifier: Matched natural campaigns show no unique joint-frontier benefit, causal signatures fail, or the controller loses timing, verifier, failure, resource, rights, fallback, recovery, denominator, expiry, or scope discipline.
